title: Graduate QA Test Engineer
description: **Role overview** Kick\-start your career in software quality at DOCOsoft, a Dublin\-based InsurTech company whose claims management platform processes billions in data for some of the world's largest insurers. As a Graduate QA Engineer, you will learn to break complex systems before customers ever see them, build automation from day one, and grow into a well\-rounded engineer with exposure across the full software development lifecycle. You will join a collaborative QA team that plays a critical role in every release. Working alongside developers, business analysts, and DevOps engineers, you will analyse requirements, design and execute tests, develop automation, and champion quality across the organisation. At DOCOsoft, no two days look the same and we actively encourage our people to contribute ideas, challenge assumptions, and explore different areas of the business. **About DOCOsoft** DOCOsoft is the leading provider of claims management software to the London insurance market. Our platform is trusted by over 20 of the world's top\-tier insurers, handling more than £10 billion in claims annually. Founded in Dublin in 2008, we now employ over 100 people across a range of disciplines and are in an exciting growth phase \- launching our new SaaS platform, DOCOsoft Vew, and expanding into international markets. Our culture is built on five core values: Collaborative, Committed, Trusted, Creative, and Grounded. We combine Irish hard work and community spirit with a philosophy of continuous improvement, and we invest in our people so they can do their best work. **Responsibilities** * Test and validate our platform to ensure it meets the highest quality standards before every customer release. * Design, write, and maintain automated test suites using Playwright. * Analyse requirements and user stories to create detailed, traceable test cases. * Investigate, reproduce, and clearly document defects so development teams can resolve issues efficiently. * Collaborate with developers, product owners, and DevOps engineers across multiple project teams. * Propose and pilot improvements to our testing processes, tools, and overall quality strategy. * Contribute to API and database\-level testing to ensure data integrity across our integrations. * Manage your time effectively across concurrent projects, communicating progress and risks proactively. **What You'll Learn in Your First Year:** We are investing in your growth from day one. In your first twelve months, you can expect to: * Gain hands\-on experience building Playwright automation suites on real client projects. * Develop a strong understanding of the insurance domain — how complex claims data is received, processed, and presented to end users. * Learn how CI/CD pipelines work and how quality gates protect production environments. * Pair with senior QA engineers and developers through a structured mentorship programme. * Carry out Non\-Functional testing activities (performance testing and security testing. * Participate in Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives. * Present your findings and testing insights to cross\-functional teams. * Explore other areas of the business — we encourage involvement in DevOps, product, and innovation initiatives. **Key Requirements:** We value curiosity, initiative, and a genuine passion for quality over a long list of credentials. If you recognise yourself in the following, we would love to hear from you: * A degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related discipline (or equivalent practical experience such as a coding bootcamp or strong personal projects). * 0–2 years of relevant experience — internships, college projects, and personal work all count. * A natural curiosity and a knack for spotting what others miss. * Comfort reading and writing code — you do not need to be an expert, but you are not afraid of a codebase. * Strong communication skills — you can clearly explain what is broken and why it matters. * A collaborative mindset and a willingness to both give and receive constructive feedback. We realise applying for jobs can feel daunting. Even if you do not tick every box above, we encourage you to apply \- the best candidate does not always check every single requirement. **Our Tech Stack** **You will work with the following technologies and tools:** * C\# and/or other object\-oriented programming languages. * Client\-side technologies: JavaScript and TypeScript. * Automation frameworks: Playwright, Selenium. * SQL Server and relational database querying. * Git, Azure DevOps for version control and work tracking. * Microsoft Visual Studio Code or similar IDEs. * API testing tools: Postman, REST APIs. * Load and performance testing (JMeter, K6\). * CI/CD pipelines and build automation. * Cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS). * Unit testing frameworks. * AI\-assisted development tools (e.g. GitHub Copilot). **Who we are:** DOCOsoft is a leading software and services provider to Lloyd’s of London and the broader London insurance market. Since our foundation, we have grown to become one of the leading insurance software specialists in the London Insurance Market. We are a growing team of over 100 colleagues based in Dublin, London, Tokyo, Portugal, Spain, India and Poland. **Here’s what we have to offer:** DOCOsoft aspires to be a market leader in the technology sector, and we are always looking for new ways to approach projects or improve existing content. We look to hire people that will help us achieve this with hard work, enthusiasm and an expression of their own ideas. We offer our people the opportunity to impact our growing business\- everyone’s contribution matters! A team environment that is focused on the creation and delivery of great products for our clients. Exciting challenges to grow and enhance their skills and a competitive pay and benefits package including; * + 25 days Annual Leave, + Private pension, + Bonus scheme, + Private health, + Life assurance. **Equal Opportunity Employer** DOCOsoft is committed to building an inclusive and diverse team that represents a variety of backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. We welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates, and do not discriminate on the grounds of race, religion, gender, marital or family status, age, disability, sexual orientation, membership of the travelling community or any other basis as protected by applicable law. Should you require reasonable accommodations during any stage of the recruitment process, please let us know.

title: Hydrometric Field Service Tech
description: **Company Description** NVM Limited is an Irish\-owned, ISO 9001:2015 certified company specializing in geotechnical, hydrological, and environmental monitoring services for the construction sector, government agencies, local authorities, and pharmaceutical industries. The company focuses on delivering high\-quality services and robust instrumentation designed for demanding environmental data collection and processing. Since 2004, NVM has built a reliable team with expertise in instrumentation installation, telemetry, field service, and data collection. As a Channel Partner for OTT Hydromet, NVM manages sales and service in Ireland and Northern Ireland for leading brands such as OTT, Hydrolab, Adcon Telemetry, Sutron, Lufft, and Kipp \& Zonen. This partnership is backed by over 140 years of combined experience in environmental measurement, with a core mission of monitoring what matters. **Role Description** This is a full\-time, on\-site Hydrometric Field Service Tech role based in Drogheda. The Hydrometric Field Service Tech will install, commission, and maintain hydrometric and environmental monitoring equipment at client sites, including water level, flow, rainfall, and water quality instrumentation. The role involves routine and reactive field visits, troubleshooting equipment, performing calibrations, and ensuring accurate telemetry and data transmission to central systems. The technician will collect, validate, and document environmental data, prepare field reports, and communicate findings and issues to the project team and clients. Daily work will also include adhering to health and safety procedures, managing tools and spare parts, and collaborating with colleagues to support project delivery and continuous service improvement. **Qualifications** * Technical skills in hydrometric or environmental instrumentation, including installation, commissioning, calibration, and basic fault\-finding on sensors and telemetry systems. * Field operations experience, such as working outdoors in varied weather, performing site surveys, using hand and power tools safely, and following robust health, safety, and environmental procedures. * Data and IT capabilities, including basic data logging and validation, familiarity with telemetry or remote monitoring systems, and competence with standard office and reporting software. * Planning and communication skills, including the ability to organize site visits, keep accurate records, write clear field reports, and communicate effectively with team members, contractors, and clients. * Relevant education or training, such as a qualification in engineering, environmental science, instrumentation, or a related technical discipline, or equivalent field experience. * Full driving license and willingness to travel regularly to sites across Ireland and Northern Ireland, including occasional early starts or extended days as required. * Ability to work both independently and as part of a small team, with a proactive, problem\-solving mindset and a focus on reliability and quality of service.

title: IT Solutions Architect
description: ### **Job Title** IT Solutions Architect ### **Salary** €76, 202 pa (Other arrangements may apply of you are currently a serving civil or public servant) ### **Tenure** 5\-year fixed term contract ### **Work Location** Dublin office ### **Closing Date** Midnight, Thursday 2nd July 2026 ### **Role Profile** We have an exciting opportunity in SEAI for a Solutions Architect in the IT Department which is part of the Corporate Services Directorate. The IT Department provides support across all aspects of SEAI operations, with teams focused on several areas: IT Project and Business System Programmes (PMO), Enterprise Architecture, Data Governance, IT Operations including IT Helpdesk, IT Governance, IT Change Management, and Cyber and Information Security. The successful candidate will join the current Enterprise Architect and support them in defining, maintaining, and governing the SEAIs IT systems architecture to ensure that business strategy objectives, and costeffective technology are aligned. Reporting to the Chief Information Officer (CIO), the successful candidate will work across the IT Department and third\-party vendors to develop and integrate ICT systems, reviewing existing systems to identify areas for improvement and monitoring the outcome of new and upgrades business systems. The successful candidate will join an enthusiastic and innovative team who are committed to the high\-quality delivery of SEAI's ambitious ICT Strategy and customer centric digital services. ### **Essential Requirements** The successful candidate must be able to demonstrate: * A degree (minimum Level 8 on the NFQ or higher) in IT, Computer Science, or other relevant discipline. * A minimum of 5 years directly relevant experience working in IT Architecture design and implementation within large complex organisations (a master's degree may account for 1\-years' experience). * Demonstrated proficiency in system architecture principles and frameworks, software development practices, cloud platforms (Azure) and service orientated architecture (SOA), data architecture and software development practices. * Experience in establishing and operating architecture frameworks and governance**.** * Significant experience in stakeholder management involving a diverse range of internal and external stakeholders with complex interdependencies. * Proficiency in development methodologies such as DevOps. ### **Desirable Requirements** * Certificate in architecture frameworks such as TOGAF. * Experience in enterprise\-level systems design. * Experience in microservices and API first architectures. * Proficiency in the Irish Language. * Experience or knowledge of delivering positive customer experiences. ### **Eligibility to Work** The SEAI has a legal obligation to ensure that all employees are lawfully entitled to work in Ireland. To qualify, candidates must hold a valid visa on the date of application and where applicable to support a candidate's application, candidates must submit a valid work permit/visa confirming permission to work in Ireland when requested. Failure to submit the required evidence, and failure to hold a valid permit/visa during the period of the contract, will result in the application and/or contract of employment being rendered void. Note in respect of UK citizens: Information regarding the Common Travel Area is available **here**. **\#LI\-Hybrid**

title: IT Support Engineer - Level 2
description: **PFH Technology are currently looking for an experienced L2 IT Support Engineer to join our team in Dublin.** **Contract:** Permanent contract **Onsite**: 4 days per week onsite, work from home on Fridays **Location:** Dublin 1, with free onsite parking Prerequisites: ITIL V4, Microsoft Certifications including MD 102 **Skills/Requirements** * **Technical Skills** * **Operating Systems:** Advanced knowledge of various operating systems like Windows, macOS, and Linux. + Installing, configuring, and troubleshooting OS\-related issues. + Knowledge of Active Directory (AD), Group Policies, and domain\-related issues (for Windows environments). * **Network Troubleshooting:** + In\-depth understanding of networking concepts such as DNS, DHCP, TCP/IP, subnetting, VLANs, and firewalls. + Diagnosing and resolving issues related to routers, switches, firewalls, and VPNs. + Monitoring and maintaining network performance. * **Hardware and Software Troubleshooting:** + Ability to diagnose hardware failures (laptops, desktops, servers). + Knowledge of software deployment, updates, and patch management. * **Virtualization:** Familiarity with virtualization platforms like VMware, Hyper\-V, or VirtualBox. * **Cloud Technologies:** Understanding cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and managing cloud services like virtual machines, storage, and cloud backups. * **Scripting and Automation:** Basic scripting knowledge (PowerShell, Bash, Python) for automating routine tasks and troubleshooting complex issues. * **Advanced Troubleshooting and Problem Solving** * **Root Cause Analysis:** Ability to perform deep dives into issues to identify root causes rather than simply resolving symptoms. * **Log Analysis:** Reading and interpreting system and network logs to diagnose and resolve technical issues. * **System Performance Tuning:** Diagnosing performance bottlenecks in hardware, applications, and networks, and suggesting improvements. * **Incident Management:** Following standard ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library) practices for incident escalation, documentation, and resolution. * **Communication Skills** * **Customer Service:** Communicating complex technical information in a user\-friendly way to non\-technical staff or clients. * **Collaboration:** Working effectively with Level 1 (frontline) support teams, and escalating issues to Level 3 or specialized support teams when necessary. * **Documentation:** Creating and updating technical documentation, including knowledge base articles and standard operating procedures (SOPs). * **Ticket Management:** Using ticketing systems (e.g., Jira, ServiceNow, Zendesk) to manage and resolve technical support requests. * **Security Awareness** * **Cybersecurity Knowledge:** Awareness of security best practices, threat mitigation, and basic concepts like encryption, VPNs, multi\-factor authentication (MFA), and firewalls. * **Endpoint Security:** Ensuring that workstations, laptops, and mobile devices are secured against potential threats. * **Backup and Disaster Recovery:** Familiarity with backup solutions, disaster recovery planning, and ensuring data integrity. * **Database Management** * **Basic SQL Knowledge:** Understanding of databases and SQL queries for troubleshooting database\-related issues or working with database administrators (DBAs). * **Backup and Restore:** Ability to perform basic database backups and restore operations in case of failure. * **Project Support** * **Support for IT Projects:** Participating in IT projects such as software rollouts, infrastructure upgrades, or migrations. * **Change Management:** Understanding change control processes and ensuring that system changes are well\-documented and implemented according to organizational policies. * **Remote Support Tools** * **Remote Troubleshooting:** Proficiency with tools like TeamViewer, AnyDesk, or Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) for assisting users remotely. * **Monitoring Tools:** Familiarity with network and system monitoring tools (e.g., Nagios, SolarWinds, PRTG) to proactively identify issues. * **Time Management and Prioritization** * **Task Prioritization:** Ability to prioritize tasks based on the severity and impact of technical issues. * **Multitasking:** Managing multiple incidents or service requests at the same time while ensuring timely resolution. * **Vendor and Third\-Party Support** * **Vendor Management:** Liaising with third\-party vendors for escalated technical issues related to specific hardware or software products. * **Warranty and RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) Management:** Handling warranty claims and equipment replacements through vendor support. * **Certifications:** **Essential** * **Microsoft Certifications:** (e.g., Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator, Microsoft 365 Certified). * **ITIL Foundation V4:** For understanding IT service management (ITSM) processes. **Optional but Beneficial** * **CompTIA Network\+, CompTIA Security\+** for networking and security fundamentals. * **Cisco Certifications:** (e.g., CCNA for networking fundamentals). **Education** * Third Level Education Qualification is preferable. PFH Technology, owned by Ricoh – a Japanese based global ICT company, is a premier provider of end\-to\-end ICT solutions and a managed services portfolio scaling from SMEs to large Enterprise organisations. We have unrivalled vendor relationships. We can procure, design, deploy and support all your ICT needs. Our ISO certified Custodian™ Cloud Services and Custodian™ Managed Services provide the technology and expertise to mitigate risk and reduce your costs immediately. We have a nationwide network of over 750 dedicated professionals, ready to meet your ICT needs, with offices in Dublin, Cork and Galway. IND1

title: Immigration Legal Executive

We empower one another to lead with confidence, creativity, and a solutions\-focused mindset. * Accountable to our customers We are dedicated to our customers and take our commitments seriously. title: Integration Architect – BTP IS - Ireland
description: **Role** – Principal Consultant **Technology** – Integration Architect – BTP IS **Location** – Dublin, Ireland **Compensation** – Competitive (including bonus) **Job Description** Today, the corporate landscape is dynamic and the world ahead is full of possibilities! None of the amazing things we do at Infosys would be possible without an equally amazing culture, the environment where ideas can flourish and where you are empowered to move forward as far as your ideas will take you. At Infosys, we assure that your career will never stand still, we will inspire you to build what’s next and we will navigate further together. Our journey of learnability, values and trusted relationships with our clients continue to be the cornerstones of our organization and these values are upheld only because of our people. **Your Role** As SAP BTP Integration Suite – Integration Architect, you will be responsible for designing, implementing, and governing integration solutions using SAP BTP Integration Suite within a RISE with SAP – S/4HANA Private Cloud environment for large Retail / Wholesale Grocery transformation programs. You will ensure SAP BTP Integration Suite is used strategically and optimally as a central integration platform for SAP to SAP integrations \& as a pass through and mediation layer for SAP\-to\-non SAP integrations, handing off orchestration to enterprise EIP platforms. This role works closely with Functional Integration Leads, Enterprise Architects, and platform teams to deliver scalable, cloud compliant, and future ready integration solutions. **Key Responsibilities** * Design and architect integrations using SAP BTP Integration Suite aligned with RISE with SAP Private Cloud principles. * Define integration patterns, naming conventions, and reusable artifacts following SAP best practices. * Ensure adherence to Clean Core, side by side extensibility, and cloud first integration strategies. * Define Platform Usage Strategy for SAP to SAP Integrations \& SAP \-to\-Non SAP . * Implement API led architecture (System, Process, Experience APIs). * Design event driven integration solutions for high volume retail scenarios. * Maintain integration documentation, interface catalogs, and run books. **Skills** **Must have** * Experience in integration and SAP landscape * Strong hands on experience with SAP BTP Integration Suite * Proven role as Integration Architect in S/4HANA programs * Experience with APIs, events, and cloud integration patterns * Strong analytical and design skills * Ability to explain complex integration designs to non technical stakeholders * Collaborative mindset across functional and technical teams * Quality and governance focused approach **Nice to have** * Experience in Retail / Wholesale Grocery or high volume domains * Expertise in APIs, events, and cloud integration patterns * Exposure to global rollouts and multi region integrations * Strong understanding of RISE with SAP architecture, especially Private Cloud Edition * Knowledge of side by side extensibility using SAP BTP **Overview** Infosys is a global leader in next\-generation digital services and consulting. We enable clients in more than 50 countries to navigate their digital transformation. With over four decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through their digital journey. We do it by enabling the enterprise with an AI\-powered core that helps prioritize the execution of change. We also empower the business with agile digital at scale to deliver unprecedented levels of performance and customer delight. Our always\-on learning agenda drives their continuous improvement through building and transferring digital skills, expertise, and ideas from our innovation ecosystem. **All aspects of employment at Infosys are based on merit, competence and performance. We are committed to embracing diversity and creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Infosys is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.**

title: Junior Associate - Investment Servicing
description: Summary: The Junior Associate will assist the Middle Office team in the development of large\-scale projects for high profile platforms, from Management Companies to fund clients and work in a supportive role within the Trade Execution and Investment Servicing teams. * Adhere to and follow the middle office checklist daily, including but not limited to Corporate Actions, Data Checks, Subscriptions and Redemption activity, Unfilled Order and Pending Instructions, Failed Trades, New Order Instructions and Reconciliations. * Assist the Manager with the design and implementation of internal and operating controls (especially in relation to OMS and PMS systems and all interactions with the market. * As you grown, learn to evaluate the risk involved in each situation, prioritize risk levels and escalate as appropriate. * Assist the team in ensuring all securities are settled on time and failed trades are investigated. * Currency Hedging including the daily production of the hedge fund workbook for review by the supervisor and instruction of hedge adjustments required which must be approved by supervisor. * Daily reconciliation between Middle Office records and depositary/custodian holdings. * Liaise daily with the trade execution team to ensure the trade execution, settlement and reconciliation process is adhered to. * Follow all pre\-trade compliance procedures and checks, escalate breaches. * Assist in the on\-boarding of new clients, broker and depositary/custodians. * As instructed by your client and approved by the supervisor, structure and process all payments across multiple platforms and service providers. * Ensure all corporate actions (elective and mandatory) are processed and accounted for. * Actively participate in fund governance meetings and attend technical training. * Assists colleagues when capacity permits and notifies supervisor with availability. * Foster positive team environment and ensure open communication. * Actively participate in team projects as instructed by the supervisor. * Develop and maintain detailed knowledge of Waystone operations and the regulatory implications thereof. * Adhere to Waystone change management procedures in order to ensure efficient implementations and minimal client impact. * Assist the team in the development, implementation and monitoring of policies and procedural manuals that contribute to the efficiency of the department while also ensuring timely and accurate delivery of service and regulatory compliance. * Ensure all regulatory and compliance requirements are met for an assigned portfolio of clients. * Communicate with clients to ensure satisfaction with the service provided and to resolve any potential issues. * When instructed, independently liaise with fund service providers and fund legal counsels. To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. description: **About Marex** Marex Group plc (NASDAQ: MRX) is a diversified global financial services platform providing essential liquidity, market access and infrastructure services to clients across energy, commodities and financial markets. The group provides comprehensive breadth and depth of coverage across four core services: clearing, agency and execution, market making, and hedging and investment solutions. It has a leading franchise in many major metals, energy and agricultural products, with access to 60 exchanges. The group provides access to the world's major commodity markets, covering a broad range of clients that include some of the largest commodity producers, consumers and traders, banks, hedge funds and asset managers. With more than 40 offices worldwide, the group has over 2,300 employees across Europe, Asia and the Americas. For more information visit https://www.marex.com/ **Role Summary** Are you an ambitious, proactive individual with a strong interest in sales and the energy industry? We are seeking a driven talent to join our team as Junior Energy Broker. In this role, you will be trained to build relationships and arrange transactions between Marex's clients in European power and gas. This is an excellent opportunity for a sales\-driven individual to develop expertise in energy brokerage while making a significant impact on the marketplace. **Responsibilities:** * Engage in arranging transactions in energy commodities, focusing on European power \& gas. * Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, including banks, hedge funds, trading houses, and corporates in the European market. * Support the onboarding of new clients and ensure ongoing relationship management. * Assist in client communication, providing regular updates and ensuring satisfaction. * Develop strategies to increase profit and growth by analyzing market trends and products. * Contribute to process improvements and assist in the development of new frameworks. * Collaborate with internal teams, including Operations and Technology, to support trading functions. * Ensure compliance with regulatory standards. * Uphold high standards of integrity and professionalism in line with company values and policies. * Ensure that you are fully aware of and adhere to internal policies that relate to you, your role or any other activities for which you have any level of responsibility * Report any breaches of policy to Compliance and/ or your supervisor as required * Escalate risk events immediately * Provide input to risk management processes, as required. * Experience/knowledge of working in a regulated financial services environment with a good knowledge of the risk and compliance requirements associated with this. ***The Company may require you to carry out other duties from time to time*** **Competencies:** * Resilient in a challenging, fast\-paced environment. * Strategic collaborator with insight and agility, able to anticipate future challenges, ensuring operational title: Junior Marketing Executive

producing video content, from filming through to editing and final delivery * Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the confidence to engage professionally with customers and stakeholders where required * Willingness and flexibility to travel for events and business activities as required * Excellent organisational skills with a high level of attention to detail and the title: Junior Pilot Recruitment Officer

Based in our Dublin HQ, this role reports to the Pilot Recruitment Manager. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent HR or Business grad to kick start their career in Recruitment. **Duties \& Responsibilities** * Organise and conduct Pilot Assessments in various locations across Europe. * Manage the A\-Z Pilot Recruitment process and application database. * Organise and attend pilot roadshows and networking events * Act as the key point of contact for all candidates and stakeholders including our partner flight schools. * Gather market intelligence and ensure Ryanair is promoted as an employer of choice. * Work closely with Ryanair’s Flight Operations and Training dept to deliver on agreed targets. * Other ad\-hoc duties as required. **Requirements** * 1 \+ years Recruitment or HR experience is desirable but not essential * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. * Excellent attention to detail * Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Office. * Good organisational and presentation skills. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Ability to work under pressure. * Efficient at multi\-tasking. * Flexibility to travel extensively. **Benefits** * Competitive salary * Discounted and unlimited travel to over 230 destinations * Defined Contribution Pension Scheme * Death in Service Benefit – Up to 2 times of annual basic salary * 20 Days Annual Leave – Increasing to 22 days after 12 months and 25 days after 3 years of continuous service. * Option for up to 5 additional unpaid leave days per year * Cycle 2 Work Scheme * Unrivalled career progression Competencies

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: At JJ Rhatigan, we have been delivering excellence in construction for over 70 years. As one of Ireland's leading building contractors, we are committed to innovation, quality, sustainability, and developing talented people who want to build rewarding careers. We are currently seeking a motivated and ambitious **Junior Quantity Surveyor** to join our commercial team in Dublin. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or an early\-career Quantity Surveyor to gain hands on experience working on some of Ireland's most prestigious construction projects while learning from experienced commercial professionals. The Role Reporting to the Commercial Manager, Managing Quantity Surveyor, or Senior Quantity Surveyor, you will support the commercial management of projects throughout their lifecycle. You will gain exposure to procurement, subcontractor management, cost reporting, valuations, contract administration, and commercial controls while contributing to the successful delivery of major construction projects. Key Responsibilities Procurement \& Tendering * Assist with take\-offs and tender documentation. * Issue tender enquiries and track tender returns. * Review quotations against drawings, specifications, and scope requirements. * Prepare tender comparisons and identify commercial risks, exclusions, and scope gaps. * Support communication with subcontractors and suppliers throughout the procurement process. Commercial Administration * Assist in preparing monthly applications and valuations. * Support the assessment of subcontractor and consultant payment applications. * Assist with payment processing through SAP. * Maintain accurate commercial records and documentation. Reporting \& Cost Control * Support the preparation of monthly commercial reports, CVRs, cashflows, and project reviews. * Assist in monitoring project costs and forecasting financial performance. * Contribute to maintaining commercial procedures and reporting deadlines. Contract \& Document Management * Maintain commercial records and project documentation on SharePoint. * Support subcontractor appointment processes and ensure documentation is complete before work commences. * Assist with variation tracking and commercial correspondence. Commercial Support * Assist with value engineering initiatives and cost\-saving opportunities. * Support dispute resolution and claims preparation when required. * Provide estimating support during tender periods as needed. What We're Looking For **Essential** * Degree in Quantity Surveying, Construction Economics, or a related discipline. * Strong numerical and analytical skills. * Excellent attention to detail and organisational abilities. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel. * Ability to work effectively as part of a team and manage multiple priorities. Desirable * Previous placement, internship, or work experience within the construction industry. * Knowledge of construction contracts and commercial processes. * Experience using SAP or similar commercial systems. What We Offer * Competitive salary and benefits package. * Structured career development and mentoring. * Exposure to large\-scale, high\-profile construction projects. * Ongoing professional development and training opportunities. * Support towards achieving professional accreditation. * Opportunity to grow your career with one of Ireland's leading construction companies. If you are looking to build a successful career in quantity surveying with a company that values excellence, collaboration, and professional growth, we would love to hear from you. **Apply today and become part of the JJ Rhatigan team.**

title: Junior Researcher (GBS HUB)
description: **Job Title** : Junior Researcher (GBS Hub) **Reporting to:** Head of Faculty **Location:** Griffith College Dublin, South Circular Road **Hours of work:** Part\-time / Fixed\-Term **Overview** GBS Hub is looking for a dynamic and innovative dissertation students to join our active projects. You will support the team with innovative EU projects and support of the GBS Research and Innovation Hub. This role will provide operational, research, reporting, and dissemination support across the AI2MED and AI4VET projects. The postholder will support the delivery of project events, reporting obligations, dissemination activities, and participant evaluation processes, ensuring project outputs are delivered on time and in accordance with funding requirements. **Job Responsibilities** * Attend project webinars, workshops, and consortium activities. * Draft quarterly internal EU project reports for review and approval. * Draft press releases and disseminate promotional materials relating to project activities. * Support organisation and delivery of the January AI2MED conference and hosting of project partners at Griffith College Dublin. * Coordinate logistics for incubation labs, bootcamps, and project events. * Collect, analyse, and collate participant feedback from project activities. * Maintain project records and support reporting requirements. * Assist with dissemination, communication, and stakeholder engagement activities. **Person Specification** * Degree or postgraduate qualification in a relevant discipline. * Strong research, writing, and analytical skills. * Excellent organisational and administrative abilities. * Experience preparing reports and communications materials. * Ability to work independently while contributing effectively to project teams. * Interest in AI, innovation, entrepreneurship, or European\-funded projects. **About Griffith College** Griffith College is a welcoming, ambitious and people\-focused institution where the quality of the working environment is shaped as much by the people as by the purpose. Across our campuses in Dublin, Cork and Limerick, colleagues work in supportive teams where professionalism, mutual respect and collaboration are part of everyday life. Founded in 1974, Griffith College is Ireland’s largest independent third\-level institution, with a community of over 8,000 students across its campuses. The College delivers a broad range of undergraduate, postgraduate and professional programmes across disciplines including business, law, computing, journalism, design and pharmaceutical science. What sets Griffith apart is not only its commitment to high\-quality education, but the culture that underpins it. Staff regularly speak about the strength of the relationships they build here, the trust between colleagues, the willingness to support one another, and the opportunity to contribute to an organisation that has meaningful impact on students from Ireland and internationally, working alongside people who genuinely care about what they do and who they work with. **Our Vision** *"To be recognised among the leading, innovative and socially enterprising of Ireland’s universities”* **Why work for Griffith College?** * Competitive salary package * Access to healthcare plans for employees and their families * Enhanced maternity leave pay * Subsidised College programmes to support career development and upskilling. * Opportunities for staff mobility through Erasmus\+ and other international mobility programmes * Mental Health and Wellbeing supports available * Free weekly yoga classes * Access to onsite fitness equipment * Availability of green space on campus, accessible to staff for sports and recreational activities * Cycle to Work scheme * Vibrant, multidisciplinary and multicultural learning environment * Inclusive and diverse community * Innovative and agile place to work * Welcoming environment committed to both learner and staff development * Staff discounts and offers from a wide range of retailers and service providers * Preferential accommodation rates during the summer period for staff, and for friends and family of staff at **Griffith Rooms** * Year\-round staff social and wellbeing events, including BBQs, coffee mornings, wellness talks, EDI events, and more * Onsite subsidised restaurant * Free parking facilities *This job description is not to be regarded as exclusive or exhaustive. It is intended as an outline of proposed areas of activity and will be amended in the light of the changing environment within the Faculty and the College.* *Griffith College is an equal opportunities employer. All candidates applying for teaching posts must undergo a mock lecture before being interviewed; will be asked to demonstrate ability via portfolio.*

title: Maintenance Technician
description: At Guerbet, we build lasting relationships so that to enable people to live better. This is Our Purpose. This is not a requirement but would be advantageous. * Time served craft qualification in: * Mechanical / Electrical / Instrumentation. * 5 Years post qualification experience. * Experience in Process Instrumentation installation, maintenance \& calibration. * City \& Guilds Qualification (or higher) in electrical / instrumentation \& control. * Instrumentation \& Control System experience. * Maintenance and Calibration experience. * Experience of Process Facilities

title: Management Trainee Hospitality (m/f/d) Dublin
description: **Our heart beats turquoise.** Working at Motel One is like coming home to friends, where you can be your true self and where appreciation and support matter. We meet on an equal footing and see ourselves as one team: from our management to our trainees. We create a home away from home for our guests with our shared passion for modern design and high\-quality service. Our more than 100 hotels in 13 countries impress with exclusive design, high service standards and first\-class inner\-city locations at an attractive price. As the founder and pioneer of the budget design concept, we are successfully expanding in the metropolises of Europe and beyond. Become part of our team and develop Motel One with us. **Job Description** Grow with us! You’re not looking for just any job after graduation? You want to shape your future, learn, and take on responsibility as a future Hotel Manager (m/f/d) – while bringing your passion for hospitality into the role? Then our Management Trainee Program is the perfect stepping stone for a career with long\-term perspective at the Motel One Group. To optimally prepare you for your future role as Hotel Manager, you will rotate between 3 hotels (national and international) over 12–18 months, gaining hands\-on experience in all operational departments. Additional projects and guidance from experienced mentors will help you develop your full potential and expand your expertise. What to expect – Your path to Hotel Management: * **Training\-on\-the\-Job:** You will rotate through all operational departments – from Front Office to Housekeeping to F\&B Service – and learn what true hospitality means. * **Learning by Doing:** You will actively contribute to daily operations, identify opportunities for improvement, and help shape the guest experience. * **Leadership Development:** You will gain insight into leadership roles, support and observe managers, assist with team coordination and motivation, and take on your own leadership projects. * **Mentoring \& Feedback:** A mentor will support you throughout the entire program and beyond. A personal development plan and regular feedback sessions will help you grow. * **Training \& Networking:** You will attend internal training sessions and workshops at our Motel One Campus, build your professional network, and prepare for your future leadership role in the hotel industry. **Qualifications** What you bring: * A completed degree in Hospitality / Tourism / Business Administration or a comparable diploma from a tourism school. * At least 1 year of operational experience in various hotel department; initial leadership experience preferred. * Flexibility regarding location and willingness to travel. * You want to take on responsibility, lead by example, motivate your team, and make bold decisions. Driven by your passion for hospitality and your ambition to become a Hotel Manager you are ready for growing into a leadership role. * Strong social skills, an open and communicative personality, and a positive attitude. * Analytical thinking, strong quality awareness, and a high level of service orientation. * A structured, independent, and team\-oriented working style – because we achieve our goals together! What we offer: * Upon successful completion of the program, you will be promoted to a leadership position. * A competitive salary, housing allowance for rotation assignments, and coverage of travel and accommodation expenses to the Motel One Campus in Munich. * Attractive benefits offered by the Motel One Group – more information available on our careers page. Are you ready for improving the world of hospitality? Then apply now for our Management Trainee Program and kick\-start your career with the Motel One Group!

and techniques. * Collaborate with team members to develop and execute creative marketing strategies. * Work closely with our automation lead on client funnels, nurture sequences and reporting in the title: Mathematics Expert (Masters/PhDs)

title: Occupational Therapist
description: **Occupational Therapist** **Salary €46,000 \-€77,000 per annum.** **Shift and schedule: Full\-time (37\.5 hours per week). Part\-time hours are also considered (minimum 3 days per week).** **Location: Gorey, County Wexford.** **Why Work with Evolve Ireland?** Be part of a supportive, purpose\-driven team dedicated to transforming neurodevelopmental care in Ireland and beyond. At Evolve, we offer: * A values\-led culture where clinical excellence, compassion, and staff wellbeing come first. * Continued professional development, clinical supervision, and reflective practice to support your ongoing clinical growth. * Competitive salary based on experience, with annual salary reviews and pension contributions. * Generous leave entitlement, including 28 days annual leave plus bank holidays \- and a day off for your birthday. * Private healthcare to support your physical and mental health * A company laptop and on\-call IT support to enable seamless remote and hybrid working. * Opportunities to contribute beyond clinical work via the Evolve Skills \& Research Academy: training, research \& content development. * Company events and a collaborative, multidisciplinary team passionate about improving outcomes for neurodivergent individuals and families in Ireland. **The Role** At Evolve Psychology Ireland, we equip our clinicians with cutting\-edge tools and support so they can focus on what matters most: delivering exceptional care to children, young people, and their families. You’ll join a passionate and supportive multidisciplinary team dedicated to making a meaningful difference in the lives of children and young people. Our service specialises in high\-quality, evidence\-based assessments for autism, ADHD, intellectual disability, communication and coordination difficulties under the Assessment of Need pathway, alongside private work. As an Occupational Therapist, you’ll contribute to comprehensive neurodevelopmental assessments, helping ensure timely, accurate diagnoses that lead to appropriate support and improved outcomes for families. **As an Occupational Therapist, you will be responsible for:** * Conducting **face\-to\-face structured observations** as part of the neurodevelopmental Assessment of Need process. * Conducting **remote developmental history interviews** with parents and carers. * Participating collaboratively in **peer MDT diagnostic discussions**, supported by senior clinicians. * Delivering clear, compassionate **feedback** to families. * Producing accurate **clinical reports**, including **diagnostic formulations**, with the help of our smart tools. * Working efficiently to meet service **Key Performance Indicators** (KPIs). * Delivering **clinical supervision** (where applicable). * Able to travel to clinic base(s) to conduct in\-person assessments (hybrid role). **Our Ways of Working** * **Digital workflows and smart report\-writing** reduce admin time and enhance clinical impact. * **A custom\-built electronic health record system,** developed in\-house using market leading research * **Continuously enhancing internal systems**, based on employee feedback to improve efficiency * **Ongoing training and onboarding** to ensure confidence in all tech and systems * **In\-house IT and operational support**, so you're never left troubleshooting alone **Qualifications and Experience** * BSc in Occupational Therapy (or equivalent) with current CORU registration as an Occupational Therapist. * Minimum of 3 years’ experience conducting diagnostic assessments for autism and/or ADHD, or other neurodevelopmental conditions, particularly in children and/or young people. * Strong understanding of differential diagnosis in neurodevelopmental presentations. * In\-depth knowledge of DSM\-5 diagnostic criteria (or equivalent) for autism, ADHD, and intellectual disability. * Trained and experienced in the use of diagnostic tools such as ADOS\-2, ADI\-R, etc * Effective time management and caseload organisation skills, with the ability to manage competing demands and meet clinical deadlines * Highly developed written language skills used to produce high\-quality clinical reports * Well\-developed communication skills with children, young people, and their families, ensuring compassionate, clear, and professional interactions throughout the assessment process * Ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team and participate in collaborative diagnostic discussions. * Must be eligible to work in the Republic of Ireland. **About Evolve Ireland** At Evolve Ireland, we do things differently. Founded in 2014 by Dr Laura Powling, Evolve Psychology began with a bold aim to redefine what clinical and neurodevelopmental psychology means in practice. Laura’s vision was simple yet powerful: to bring care, connection, empathy, and clinical excellence together, with no compromise. What started as a small practice delivering expert witness and neurodevelopmental assessments has grown into an international independent psychology and neurodevelopmental service providing services across the UK, Ireland and beyond. In Ireland, we are nationally and regionally approved by the HSE. We also accept privately funded referrals. We specialise in a number of different pathways which include: * Neurodevelopmental assessments through the HSE Assessment of Need pathway, * Undertaking assessments under the National Autism Framework * Undertaking assessments referred from Community Disability Network Teams (CDNTs) * Providing adult neurodevelopmental assessments * Private neurodevelopmental assessments (online when clinically appropriate and in person) * SpLD (Dyslexia and Dyscalculia) assessments * Specialist individual assessments by Occupational Therapists and Speech and Language Therapists We are clinician founded and clinician led. Our clinicians, where relevant, are proudly registered with CORU or affiliated with the Psychological Society of Ireland and the Department of Health. Globally, we are an award\-winning, multidisciplinary team of over 100 experts, committed to delivering high\-quality expert services. **What sets Evolve Ireland apart?** * Expert leadership: Founded and led by Dr Laura Powling, a Consultant Clinical Psychologist with 20\+ years’ experience across mental health, psychological, forensic and neurodevelopmental services, bringing deep clinical insight and hands\-on leadership to everything we do. * National HSE approved provider: Recognised for the high\-quality services we provide, offering services regionally and nationally across Ireland * Dedicated Clinical Leadership \- Across all of our clinical pathways * Gold Standard Assessments compliant with NICE, PSI and other national and international assessment standards * Access to digital tools to support assessment delivery, including an electronic health record system * Comprehensive CPD and discipline\-specific clinical supervision offerings * We prioritise clinical quality and real impact, not speed or scale at the expense of care. Our assessments are evidence\-based, multidisciplinary, and designed to support the whole person, not just produce a diagnosis. * We offer more than assessment work. Our clinicians can contribute to the Evolve International Skills \& Research Academy, developing training and post\-assessment support for families and educational providers, shaping neuroinclusive content, or collaborating on research, all part of our broader mission to raise awareness and improve support for neurodivergent individuals, educational institutions and workplaces. * We have a strong, values\-led culture. Compassion, clarity, and integrity guide everything we do, from our team interactions to the way we support families. * Multi\-channel employee engagement\- A dedicated, holistic approach to employee engagement and wellbeing. Including online community information forum, team meetings, company events, open feedback forums, management Q\& A sessions, employee recognition and awards. * We’re growing sustainably, with purpose. With new clinics across Ireland targeting areas of most need and hard to reach communities. This includes hybrid and online assessments, where clinically appropriate. We are also a carbon\-neutral company. Pay: €46,000\.00\-€77,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company events * On\-site parking * Private medical insurance Ability to commute/relocate: * Gorey, County Wexford: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Are you ADOS and/or ADI\-R trained? Experience: * Autism and/or ADHD assessment: 3 years (required) Licence/Certification: * CORU Registration (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Office Administrator
description: **About eScribers** Established in 2005 by court reporting professionals, eScribers has grown to become the leader in reporting and transcription services for courts and government agencies across the US and UK \& Ireland. Our company is private equity backed and growing rapidly worldwide, leading the court reporting market in technology innovations and client services. With customers across the US, UK \& Ireland, we currently hold offices in Phoenix, Virginia, Maryland, London, Dublin and Israel. At eScribers we value professionalism, innovative thinking, and collaboration and this is what guides us in all aspects of our day\-to\-day operations. Come be a part of our growth by joining our outstanding team of professionals! **Job Brief** We are seeking an **Office Administrator** to join our amazing team in Ireland! It is a great opportunity to start your career in a growing company with many opportunities to grow and develop. This is a full\-time position where you will enjoy a dynamic work environment. Working days for this position are Monday to Friday; working hours are 12:00pm\-8:30pm, in our Dublin office. **Responsibilities:** As an **Office Administrator,** you will: * Assist with data entry tasks. * Manage office printing tasks. * Coordinate and interact with various stakeholders via email and phone. * E\-filing/docketing transcripts for various courts when needed. * Provide general administrative support to the office * Download and/or upload files into our workflow systems. * Provide support to other teams, as needed. **Requirements:** We are looking for an energetic, detail\-oriented, organized, self\-starting professional with the following: * Minimum one year of prior experience working with general computer programs: Word, Excel, Adobe/PDF, email, and data entry is required. * A good understanding of the internet environment is required. * Proficient in English and excellent writing skills, spelling, punctuation, and English grammar. * Proficient phone skills. * Ability to multitask and work on multiple project deadlines simultaneously. * Eligible to work in Ireland. * Experience in a legal office environment is a plus but not required. title: Onboarding Consultant I, Guest

title: Operational Risk Associate
description: Pantheon has been at the forefront of private markets investing for more than 40 years, earning a reputation for an innovative approach to investing in secondaries, co\-investments, and primary fund investments, as well as capital formation across commingled funds, evergreen vehicles and customized solutions. Our specialist investment capabilities span multiple strategies across private equity, infrastructure and real assets, and private credit. Through our collaborative and committed culture, we find new ways to solve complex problems together and deliver innovative investment opportunities across private markets. Pantheon currently manages approximately $82\.3 billion in AUM across all its strategies, serving more than 750 institutional and 638 private wealth clients worldwide For further details please visit www.pantheon.com **Purpose of Role** The Operational Risk Associate will be part of the wider Enterprise Risk / Operational Risk function and support the team in further developing and managing operational risks across all business functions. The position will report to the Head of Enterprise/Operational Risk. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the development and implementation of Pantheon’s global operational risk frameworks and policies and procedures for adoption by Pantheon Group entities * Support the ISAE 3402/SSAE 16 annual processes including completion of the document, drafting of controls wording and providing external auditor with supporting information. * Support process and control reviews to identify risks and issues in Pantheon’s control environment. Reviews will include identified areas of risk, monitoring programme and new business processes. * Manage Error/Incident reporting process including reporting to management and resolution of any issues. Ensure monitoring procedures are in line with Pantheon’s risk policies. Direct investigation of serious error or breaches and make recommendations for change to management * Co\-ordinate and assist in providing risk oversight and governance across third party supplier relationships of all Pantheon Group entities * Support business continuity process including BCP and testing programme. * Support to manage various risk\-related frameworks such as the data privacy / data protection, vendor risk, Insurance risk, and assist with any client / business related queries * Support and further develop a centralised library of processes, risks and controls in a strategic risk system **Skills And Experience Required** * Degree level education * Experience of working in a risk function ideally in an investment banking, asset management, private equity environment * Understanding of investment management and products * Ability to work on own initiative and in team * Ability to identify when issues should be escalated for further discussion * Controls awareness * Keen problem\-solving skills, ability to offer solutions to issues **This job description is not to be construed as an exhaustive statement of duties, responsibilities, or requirements. You may be required to perform other job\-related duties as reasonably requested by your manager.** **Pantheon is an Equal Opportunities employer, we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce so if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't perfectly align we'd still encourage you to apply.**

title: Operations Administrator
description: At **ABB**, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world. **This position reports to:** Service Operations Manager \_\_ **Job Summary** In this role, you will work with the Operations Manager to provide general administrative services at the business level and provide back\-up support within the Operations team within Electrification Service (ELSE) division. This role will be a 12 month fixed term contract. **Our Team Dynamics** Our teams support each other, collaborate, and never stop learning. Everyone brings something unique, and together we push ideas forward to solve real problems. Being part of our team means your work matters \- because the progress we make here creates real impact out there. **What’s in it for you?** We want you to bring your full self to work—your ideas, your energy, your ambition. You’ll have the tools and freedom to grow your skills, shape your path, and take on challenges that matter. Here, your work creates impact you can see and feel, every day. **Your Role and Responsibilities** In this role, you’ll help run what runs the world, by taking on meaningful work that drives real impact. *Work model: \#Hybrid from our Dublin office* * Performs general administrative activities and provides backup support to the team in case of sickness/absence/workload. * Acts as local support for all administration activities within ELSE Ireland. * Schedules and coordinates training for the Field Service Engineers to enable them to proceed to a customer visit safely. * Schedules Inductions and access to ABB Customer sites * Builds and maintains strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders and effectively communicates with all stakeholders. **Qualifications for the Role** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in a business administration support role or similar with proven ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities * Excellent attention to detail, ability to maintain accurate records, and proficiency with office management systems. * Competent use of Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook), knowledge of SAP advantageous * Strong written and verbal communication abilities in English, with capability to interact professionally with all levels of staff. * Ability to identify issues, take initiative, work independently, and contribute ideas for improving operational efficiency. **Benefits** As part of our dedication to our employees, ABB Ireland offers an attractive array of benefits which include * Competitive Salary * Private Medical Allowance for Yourself and Family * Generous Bonus Scheme * Competitive Contributory Pension Scheme * Employee Share Acquisition Plan * Open Job Market (Internal Mobility Opportunities, Mentorships \& Access To Projects Globally) * Employee Assistance Programme * Generous Employee Referral Scheme * 25 Days Annual Leave Plus Bank Holidays **More about us** Join ABB Ireland and be part of a dynamic team that is making a real difference. We offer a supportive and inclusive work environment where you can grow your career, develop your skills, and contribute to impactful work that matters. Together, we are building a sustainable future for generations to come. **More about us** ABB's Service Division partners with our customers to improve the availability, reliability, predictability and sustainability of electrical products and installations. The Division’s extensive service portfolio offers product care, modernization, and advisory services to improve performance, extend equipment lifetime and deliver new levels of operational and sustainable efficiency. Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences. Ready to make an impact? title: Operations Specialist (Systems & Data) - 12 month fixed term contract
description: **Job Description** *We are currently looking for a detail\-oriented and motivated Systems Operations Specialist to join our Fund Managers team.* *As an Operations Specialist, you’ll collaborate with colleagues across our global offices while gaining hands\-on experience in working in a fast\-paced environment.* *The ideal candidate will possess an understanding of the financial industry and demonstrate a strong technical aptitude and a keen interest in data management.* ***IMPORTANT: Experience working with Investran or similar private equity/investment management software is preferred.*** **Your Core Responsibilities Will Include** * Support day\-to\-day management of the operational fund business of LGT Fund Managers; title: Operations Supervisor
description: * **Duration: 12 Month Fixed term contract** * **Location: Citywest** **The role** This role is to ensure that the welfare of the people, plant and equipment are managed correctly to ensure we deliver a best\-in\-class service to all our customers in a cost\-effective way. The successful applicant will be responsible for the execution of all the activities required to manage the operational responsibilities across Inbound (receiving, put away/replenishment) or Outbound (pick, pack and ship) operations in the Warehouse. Responsible for assisting in the staffing, training, and scheduling of the warehouse team, while maintaining strict adherence to planning hours, warehouse inventory and to achieve productivity goals. **Key Outcomes** * Shift Performance: allocate resources to ensure all key areas are manned sufficiently to deliver the operational performance required by the business. * Provide hands\-on supervision for staff to maximise productivity and accuracy in their job functions. * Manage and drive the hourly targets set in each area and ensure that these are achieved and report on any exceptions to Management. * Effectively utilising Warehouse Management and Control Systems to monitor, manage and troubleshoot shift performance. * Ability to understand warehouse management systems and operating principles to drive operational performance. * Proactive decision making in relation to interpretation of the warehouse performance data. * Maintain a safe work environment and ensure hygiene needs and regulations are adhered to in line with GDP guidelines. * Safety Compliance: Ensure strict adherence to safety regulations and best practices, creating a secure workspace for all warehouse staff. * Ensure Quality Management and Standard Operating Procedures are adhered to at all times. * Ensure that time and attendance records for your team are accurate and up to date on a weekly basis. * Supervise daily processes to ensure all Key Performance Indicator (KPI) metrics are met or exceeded. * Assist in the training and development of the warehouse team. * Monitor and enhance individual performance through clearly defined expectations, training, coaching and mentoring to achieve desired results. * Monitor the performance of the process and the team and report on same. * Take an active part in the development of new processes in the business to drive efficiency and productivity across the site. * Ensure high standards of communication in all interaction in line with Company Dignity and Respect at Work policy. * Ensure smooth and efficient handover of tasks with incoming Operations Supervisor. * Must be available to work weekends. **Key Skills \& Experiences** * You will need experience in a similar position, ideally in FMCG * Proven experience working in a dynamic fast\-paced environment managing large scale diverse teams. * Excellent Leadership and Interpersonal skills and the ability to work at different levels in the organisation, inside and outside of the operations function. * Very strong communication skills to deal on a regular basis with staff at all levels of the organisation. * Proven capability to implement operational improvements to enhance service and productivity. * Experience in coaching and developing teams to deliver superior results. * Staff management, motivation and influencing skills. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Computer skills; with experience and knowledge of Warehouse Management Software, Excel and Word. * Strong data analytical skills and the ability to interpret data. * In depth knowledge of health and safety regulations and best practices **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. title: Operations Supervisor
description: * Duration: 12 Month Fixed term contract * Location: Citywest **The role** This role is to ensure that the welfare of the people, plant and equipment are managed correctly to ensure we deliver a best\-in\-class service to all our customers in a cost\-effective way. The successful applicant will be responsible for the execution of all the activities required to manage the operational responsibilities across Inbound (receiving, put away/replenishment) or Outbound (pick, pack and ship) operations in the Warehouse. Responsible for assisting in the staffing, training, and scheduling of the warehouse team, while maintaining strict adherence to planning hours, warehouse inventory and to achieve productivity goals. **Key Outcomes** * Shift Performance: allocate resources to ensure all key areas are manned sufficiently to deliver the operational performance required by the business. * Provide hands\-on supervision for staff to maximise productivity and accuracy in their job functions. * Manage and drive the hourly targets set in each area and ensure that these are achieved and report on any exceptions to Management. * Effectively utilising Warehouse Management and Control Systems to monitor, manage and troubleshoot shift performance. * Ability to understand warehouse management systems and operating principles to drive operational performance. * Proactive decision making in relation to interpretation of the warehouse performance data. * Maintain a safe work environment and ensure hygiene needs and regulations are adhered to in line with GDP guidelines. * Safety Compliance: Ensure strict adherence to safety regulations and best practices, creating a secure workspace for all warehouse staff. * Ensure Quality Management and Standard Operating Procedures are adhered to at all times. * Ensure that time and attendance records for your team are accurate and up to date on a weekly basis. * Supervise daily processes to ensure all Key Performance Indicator (KPI) metrics are met or exceeded. * Assist in the training and development of the warehouse team. * Monitor and enhance individual performance through clearly defined expectations, training, coaching and mentoring to achieve desired results. * Monitor the performance of the process and the team and report on same. * Take an active part in the development of new processes in the business to drive efficiency and productivity across the site. * Ensure high standards of communication in all interaction in line with Company Dignity and Respect at Work policy. * Ensure smooth and efficient handover of tasks with incoming Operations Supervisor. * Must be available to work weekends. **Key Skills \& Experiences** * You will need experience in a similar position, ideally in FMCG * Proven experience working in a dynamic fast\-paced environment managing large scale diverse teams. * Excellent Leadership and Interpersonal skills and the ability to work at different levels in the organisation, inside and outside of the operations function. * Very strong communication skills to deal on a regular basis with staff at all levels of the organisation. * Proven capability to implement operational improvements to enhance service and productivity. * Experience in coaching and developing teams to deliver superior results. * Staff management, motivation and influencing skills. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Computer skills; with experience and knowledge of Warehouse Management Software, Excel and Word. * Strong data analytical skills and the ability to interpret data. * In depth knowledge of health and safety regulations and best practices **Uniphar – Who We Are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties **.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: Part Time Sales Assistant
description: **Company Description** Sports Direct is the UK's No.1 sports retailer, with an unbeatable range of sportswear, footwear, and equipment at prices that keep customers coming back. With 600\+ stores and still growing, we're part of the Frasers Group: a bold, ambitious business known for shaking up the industry and creating real opportunities at every level. If you've got energy, drive, and a love for retail done at pace, you'll fit right in. **The Role: Sales Assistant** This is retail with momentum. As a Sales Assistant at Sports Direct, you're on the front line of one of the UK's busiest store networks, helping customers find what they need, keeping the floor sharp, and powering the team through every trading day. No two shifts look the same, and that's exactly the point. **What's In It For You** * **Competitive hourly rate** that rewards what you put in * **Flexible working** to fit around your life * **Staff discount** across the full Frasers Group family of brands * **Career progression** in a fast\-growing retail business that promotes from within * **A fun, fast\-paced atmosphere** with a team that has your back **Job Description** **What You'll Do** * Greet customers, answer questions, and help them find the right product. * Deliver brilliant service at every touchpoint, from the door to the till. * Keep the shop floor tidy, well\-stocked, and visually on point. * Replenish shelves and rails so the floor is always ready for the next customer. * Process transactions quickly and accurately. * Promote current offers and spot smart upsell opportunities. * Work as one team to hit daily sales and service goals. **Qualifications** **What You'll Bring** * A confident, friendly, positive attitude. * Strong communication skills and a customer\-first mindset. * A team\-player mentality that thrives when the pace picks up. * Flexibility and reliability, especially through peak trade. * Motivation to deliver results and take real pride in your work. * Retail or customer service experience is a bonus, but not essential. We'll back you to learn. **Additional Information** **How We Work** Our values shape every shift, every conversation, every decision: * **Think Without Limits:** Move fast, take bold action, and bring the team with you. * **Own It and Back Yourself:** Take responsibility, master the basics, and drive results. * **Be Relevant:** Stay connected to our people, our partners, and the planet. Interest in these roles is high, so we may close the vacancy early. Get your application in soon to avoid missing out. **Apply now** and join a winning team at Sports Direct, where passion meets performance.
