title: Partner Designate | Utilities & Energy Sector
description: Senior Associate – Partnership Track **Energy \& Infrastructure** **Location:** Dublin, Ireland **Start Date:** Q1 2026 **Practice Area:** Energy \& Infrastructure **About The Opportunity** Our client, a leading Irish full\-service law firm with significant market presence, is seeking an ambitious Senior Associate to join their Energy \& Infrastructure team on a clear partnership track. This is a genuine partnership opportunity for an exceptional lawyer ready to take the next step in their career. You will work alongside market\-leading partners on high\-profile mandates while building your own client base and developing the technical expertise and commercial skills essential for partnership. Ireland's energy transition presents unprecedented opportunities for legal professionals in this space. This role offers the platform, mentorship, and client exposure needed to establish yourself as a future leader in energy law. The Role As Senior Associate On Partnership Track, You Will Work On Sophisticated Energy And Infrastructure Matters With Increasing Autonomy And Client Responsibility, Including Renewable Energy \& Energy Transition * Advise on development, financing, and acquisition of renewable energy projects (wind, solar, hydrogen, battery storage) * Support clients navigating Ireland's energy transition and decarbonisation strategies * Draft and negotiate power purchase agreements (PPAs), connection agreements, and offtake arrangements * Advise on regulatory frameworks including renewable energy support schemes and grid connection processes Energy Regulation \& Compliance * Provide strategic advice on energy regulatory matters and CRU compliance * Advise energy companies on licensing requirements and regulatory authorisations * Navigate complex regulatory frameworks for electricity and gas markets * Support clients on energy policy developments and legislative changes Infrastructure \& Project Development * Work on major infrastructure projects across the energy sector * Advise on project development agreements, EPC contracts, and O\&M arrangements * Support financing structures for large\-scale energy and infrastructure projects * Handle land acquisition, planning, and environmental permitting matters Commercial Contracting * Draft and negotiate complex commercial agreements for energy sector clients * Advise on supply agreements, joint ventures, and strategic partnerships * Support M\&A activity in the energy and infrastructure sectors **Essential Requirements** * Qualified Solicitor with 6\-8\+ years PQE, ideally with energy, infrastructure, or commercial/regulatory experience * Strong technical capability in drafting and negotiating complex commercial agreements * Demonstrable interest in energy sector and understanding of energy transition challenges * Client relationship skills with ability to manage client matters independently * Business development appetite – keen to build own network and client base * Partnership ambition – clear desire to progress to partnership within 2\-4 years Ideal Candidate Profile **Technical Excellence** * Strong commercial drafting and negotiation skills * Understanding of energy regulatory frameworks (or strong regulatory background transferable to energy) * Experience with project development, power sector transactions, or infrastructure matters highly desirable * Ability to quickly master complex technical and regulatory concepts **Commercial \& Client Focus** * Practical, solutions\-oriented approach to legal advice * Ability to understand client business objectives and commercial drivers * Experience managing client relationships and running matters independently * Strong project management skills for complex, multi\-party transactions **Business Development Mindset** * Proactive approach to building professional networks * Enthusiasm for contributing to pitches, thought leadership, and sector engagement * Understanding that partnership requires both technical excellence and commercial contribution * Ambition to become recognized voice in Irish energy sector **Leadership Potential** * Ability to mentor and supervise junior lawyers * Contributing team player with collaborative approach * High standards of professional conduct and integrity * Clear communicator with clients, colleagues, and stakeholders

title: Passenger Services Agent 20hours
description: **We are currently recruiting for Passenger Service Agent to join our Front of house airport ground operations team in Dublin Airport. This frontline role is essential in delivering excellent customer service and ensuring safety, security and efficiency for all customers.** **Key Responsibilities:** * Provide exceptional customer service at check in , boarding and arrivals hall. * Assist passengers with check\-in procedures, luggage handling, and boarding processes. * Assist with passport and visa verification in accordance with airline and immigration rules. * Ability to deal with problems when they occur. * Manage check in procedures, seat allocation and issuance of boarding pass. * Handle and tag baggage and support with lost/delayed baggage. * Ensure strict adherence to safety and security protocols at all times. * Support passengers during flight delays, cancellations, or irregular operations. * Liaise with airlines, ground staff, immigration and security. * Motivated individual who thrives in a fast paced environment where no two days are the same. * Adhere to the Fenix uniform and grooming standards. **Candidate Requirements :** * Strong communication and interpersonal skills. * Previous customer service or airport experience (advantage) * Ability to remain calm and professional under pressure. * Excellent personal grooming and professional appearance. * Strong timekeeping and reliability for shift based work. * Fluent in English ( additional language an advantage) * Pass a 5 year background check and Garda Vetting process. * Eligible to live and work in the E.U. **Benefits:** * Competitive compensation package with opportunities for advancement. * Comprehensive training and ongoing support to enhance skills and knowledge. * Employee discounts in the loop! * Collaborative and inclusive work environment with opportunities for professional growth and development. * Full uniform provided * Company Pension Scheme * Free on\-site car parking Job Type: Full\-time/Part\-time\- Permanent Pay: €16\.00 per hour Job Types: Full\-time, Part\-time Pay: €16\.00 per hour Benefits: * Company pension * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Pastry Chef de Partie
description: At Dean Group, we reimagine hospitality with character, not conformity. Founded in Dublin and now growing across Europe and the US, we create hotels shaped by personality, culture, and a heart\-led hustle. Each of our hotels, restaurants, bars and gyms tells its own story, brought to life through humble hospitality and genuine human connection. Our people are at the centre of it all. They show up with individuality, warmth and curiosity, shaping who we are and how guests experience our hotels every day. We welcome fresh thinking and imagination, because playing it safe never made anything unforgettable. We are now seeking an experienced, passionate **Pastry Chef de Partie** to join the team in **Jean\-Georges at The Leinster Hotel.** This is a rare opportunity to be part of an internationally celebrated brand, within an energetic, multicultural brigade of professionals who thrive on creativity and precision. Perched atop Dublin's most exciting Art Deco landmark, The Leinster, Jean\-Georges is a Michelin\-commended, modern fusion restaurant that celebrates the best of Irish ingredients elevated with bold, imaginative techniques. Part of the world\-renowned Jean\-Georges collection \- from the two\-Michelin\-star flagship in New York to restaurants in Kyoto, London, Las Vegas and beyond \- our Dublin home is at the forefront of Ireland's culinary scene. **Key Areas** * Support the Head Pastry in creating innovative, high\-quality desserts and pastries * Oversee daily operations in the pastry section of the kitchen * Deliver consistent presentation and flavour across all pastry items * Contribute to menu development and bespoke creations for special occasions * Maintain food safety, hygiene, and quality standards within the pastry section * Supervise and guide Pastry Commis **Job Purpose** The Pastry Chef de Partie plays a central role in delivering exceptional pastry and dessert offerings at our premium hotel. This role combines creativity, technical expertise, and leadership to produce high\-quality confections that meet both guest expectations and brand standards. The Pastry Chef de Partie oversees day\-to\-day pastry operations, and ensures that the section runs smoothly, efficiently, and in compliance with all food safety regulations. **Role Responsibilities** **Pastry Production \& Presentation** * Prepare and produce a variety of pastries, desserts, and confections to the highest standards * Decorate and present pastries using artistic techniques, icings, and toppings * Create custom\-made desserts and pastries for client requests, weddings, and events **Kitchen Operations \& Stock Management** * Prepare ingredients and operate baking equipment efficiently and safely * Monitor and manage stock of pastry ingredients and equipment * Assist with ordering supplies and ensuring the pastry section is adequately stocked * Maintain cleanliness, organisation, and readiness of the pastry section at all times **Quality Control \& Compliance** * Ensure all dishes are prepared in line with food hygiene and safety standards * Conduct regular checks to maintain consistency in quality, flavour, and presentation * Uphold brand and hotel standards across all pastry offerings **Team Leadership \& Collaboration** * Supervise and train Commis Pastry providing clear guidance and feedback * Work collaboratively with the Executive Chef, Sous Chefs, and Head Pastry to support smooth kitchen operations * Foster a positive, professional, and team\-oriented working environment **Preferred Experience \& Skills** * Extensive knowledge of pastry\-making and baking techniques * Strong artistic and creative skills in dessert decoration and presentation * In\-depth knowledge of sanitation principles and food preparation standards * Experience supervising or training junior kitchen staff * Familiarity with professional baking and kitchen equipment * Excellent communication skills and enthusiasm for teamwork * Passion for pastry and a desire to innovate within the role **Benefits:** * Global discounted staff room rates across the group * 50% off Food and 25% Beverage across the group * Friends and Family discount * Gym Membership Discounts\* * Continual training and development * Career progression within the Group * Gratuities \* * Meals on shift * Bike to work scheme * Taxsaver for travel * Refer a friend scheme * Wellness at work events and access to wellness platform * Employment Assistance Programme * Group discounts for cinema tickets, shopping deals etc * Team appreciation days * Regular funfilled activities and gatherings for all teams across the group * Applicable to certain positions Dean Group owns and operates a number of hotel across Ireland, in certain instances we may feel your skills and experience may suit another role we have available. By submitting your application, you are permitting Dean Group to contact you for other opportunities within the Group \#HP

title: Payments - EMEA Subsidiary Payments Sales Associate - Dublin
description: **JOB DESCRIPTION** The EMEA Subsidiary Payments Sales (SPS) team is responsible for covering Regional Treasury Centres and subsidiaries based in Europe, the Middle East and Africa (EMEA) of J.P. Morgan's multinational client relationships. The EMEA SPS team is closely aligned with the Global Corporate Bank and various other Banking Product verticals in delivering innovative payments solutions to J.P. Morgan's clients. This includes marketing the firm's in\-country capabilities as well as growing cross\-border revenue. As an EMEA Subsidiary Payments Sales Associate (SPS) within the EMEA Subsidiary Payments Sales (SPS) team, you will work in close day\-to\-day partnership with the Ireland Subsidiary Payments Sales lead, supporting a portfolio of multinational clients with complex regional and cross\-border treasury needs. You will gain exposure to client coverage, internal stakeholder management, commercial pipeline discipline and execution of client priorities, while also being connected to the broader EMEA SPS analyst and associate community for training, knowledge\-sharing and development. This is a high\-ownership role for someone who wants to take real responsibility for client follow\-through, internal execution and portfolio discipline, while developing quickly within J.P. Morgan's Payments franchise. The role offers strong on\-the\-job learning, regular coaching and connectivity with regional teams, including potential travel for training, team engagement and internal or client meetings. The role is well suited to someone looking to build a career in Payments Sales, Client Coverage or broader transaction banking, with direct exposure to clients, senior internal stakeholders and the practical delivery of solutions across the bank. **Job Responsibilities** * Work in close partnership with the Ireland Subsidiary Payments Sales lead to support day\-to\-day client coverage, portfolio execution, internal follow\-ups, meeting preparation and post\-meeting action tracking. * Own and maintain a structured tracker of client priorities, open items, internal follow\-ups, owners, timelines and next steps across the Ireland SPS portfolio \& be a reliable point of continuity for clients and internal partners, ensuring follow\-ups are tracked, progressed and closed out with a clear answer or next step. * Participate in client meetings and routine follow\-up calls where appropriate, building confidence in client communication, issue coordination and the practical delivery of Payments solutions. * Produce high\-quality call reports, meeting notes and post\-meeting follow\-ups with clear action items, owners and deadlines. * Proactively coordinate with internal partners across Payments, Banking, Product, Implementation, Service, Operations, Credit and KYC to help progress client requests, remove execution blockers and navigate a complex matrix organisation with professionalism and good judgement. * Coordinate new client onboardings, KYC processes and implementation activity to ensure a smooth client experience and timely execution of new business \& support pipeline management and revenue discipline, including maintaining accurate opportunity updates, next steps, expected timelines and key dependencies. * Assist with Request for Proposal (RFP) and Request for Information (RFI) responses, coordinating with internal teams to complete ad\-hoc analysis including cost/benefit analysis, pricing and profitability models. * Review and analyse portfolio data to identify key trends, volume fluctuations or risk issues, and provide clear summaries with actionable recommendations to the team lead. * Develop and maintain a working knowledge of JPMorgan Payments products, pricing philosophy and billing processes, building this understanding progressively through on\-the\-job exposure and training. * Stay informed on industry developments, market themes and competitor positioning relevant to the Irish and broader EMEA client base, and bring insights to client discussions and internal planning \& Bring problems together with proposed solutions or recommended next steps, using judgement to escalate matters appropriately where timelines, ownership or client outcomes are at risk. * Engage with the broader EMEA SPS analyst and associate community, including regional training, knowledge\-sharing, team connectivity and occasional travel where required \& Use approved digital, data and AI tools responsibly to improve productivity, including research, drafting, synthesis, data organisation, meeting preparation and follow\-up discipline. **Required Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills** * Prior experience in corporate banking, transaction banking, payments, treasury services, implementation, client service, relationship management support or a similar professional services role. We value attitude, aptitude and pace of learning, alongside relevant experience. * Strong ownership mindset with the ability to independently manage follow\-ups, track open items and drive tasks through to completion. * Highly organised, detail\-oriented and reliable, with the ability to manage multiple competing priorities without losing sight of deadlines or client outcomes. * Proactive working style, with a natural tendency to look for ways to help, anticipate needs and improve processes. * Strong written communication skills, with the ability to produce clear, concise and well\-structured updates, meeting notes, briefing materials and client follow\-ups. * Comfortable operating in a complex matrix organisation and following up with stakeholders across countries, functions and seniority levels. * Fluency in English is essential. **Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills** * Curious, coachable and fast\-learning, with a genuine interest in corporate treasury, payments, multinational clients and commercial banking. * Comfortable working closely with a senior sales lead in a fast\-moving environment, with the confidence to ask questions, take feedback, anticipate needs and build increasing ownership over time. * Collaborative, resilient and approachable, with the ability to build trust across teams and remain composed when priorities, timelines or conversations are challenging. **ABOUT US** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world's most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants' and employees' religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **ABOUT THE TEAM** J.P. Morgan's Commercial \& Investment Bank is a global leader across banking, markets, securities services and payments. Corporations, governments and institutions throughout the world entrust us with their business in more than 100 countries. The Commercial \& Investment Bank provides strategic advice, raises capital, manages risk and extends liquidity in markets around the world.

title: Planning Graduate - 2026 Suir Engineering Graduate
description: We have an exciting opportunity for Planning Graduate to join our 18\-month graduate programme. Here in Suir Engineering we are a leading international provider of innovative Mechanical, Electrical and Instrumentation Engineering Solutions. Our core sectors are Energy, Power and Renewables, Pharma, Food \& Beverage, and Data Centres. We provide best in class contracting solutions with in\-house, flexible resources capable of delivering a personable and consistent service to our customers. Over the past 40 years we have delivered projects for Irish and multinational clients across the globe. We are currently expanding significantly and are delivering large scale projects across Ireland, the U.K, Germany, Denmark \& Sweden. **We Have Opportunities To Join Our Programme In** * Dublin Office * Waterford Office Travel will be required various sites as requested. **Our Graduate Programme** In addition to on the job experience we also provide some specialized programmes for our Graduate Trainees * Soft Skills Modules – communications / presentation skills * Career Pathway Conversations – learn how different parts of our business work * Group Projects \& Presentations * Site Visits **Is this Graduate Program for me?** * Level 8 or relevant bachelor’s degree in relevant field * Analytical and problem\-solving skills with attention to detail. * A proactive attitude with a willingness to learn and take on challenges. * Able to work in a team environment * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Flexibility to work on different sites and relocate if required. **What will I be doing?** * Continuously model the steps of the construction project in different diagrams. Monitor and report on project progress. * Make decisions related to logistics and resources. * Monitor \& Report on Internal productivity and issue Earned Value Analysis on a weekly basis. * Attend meetings with the Project Team to discuss progress made and any issues that arise over the course of the project. * Any other reasonable and relevant duties as requested by your manager, necessary to meet the ongoing needs of the company. * Daily \& weekly programme updates with the client. **In Return From Suir Engineering** * Salary starting from €40,000 per year * 23 days annual leave * Company Pension contributions \- no waiting periods * Paid Maternity and Paternity Leave * Private Health Insurance contribution * Life and Disability cover * EAP for employees and their families * Educational Assistance, CPD, Chartership supports and upskilling opportunities * Opportunities for international assignments to work on one of our European projects in Sweden, Denmark, or Germany. Our way of working is called the Suir Way, designed to help us on a journey of continuous improvement. * Our people are empowered to make changes to ensure a quality install through effective ways of working. * Our people have a voice and feel empowered. * We have an in\-built culture of innovation, learning and continuous improvement. * Value is delivered every step of the journey. Our efficient processes deliver value. * We have a standard, consistent and fully integrated health, safety, environment, and quality system within the business. Suir Engineering are an equal opportunities employer; we value our greatest asset …. our People. We do not require the support of Recruitment agencies at this time.

title: Porter-Mailroom Technician
description: **Role Description:** The Porter\-Mailroom Technician will be primarily responsible for assisting the Facilities Management Maintenance Team across multiple Buildings for a commercial customer. The role is based in the Sandyford/Leopardstown area. Workings hours are a 39\-hour week rostered between 7am and 6pm Mon\-Fri **Key Accountabilities:** Carry out room set ups and alterations as requested by events or customers site team. Ensure all equipment is in good working order, remove from use any damaged equipment and report defects. Ensure all equipment is safely and securely stored when not in use. Liaise with and update supervisor on progress of site activities. * Assist subcontractors as required * Assist with any queries regarding quotations and purchasing * Engage and utilise the CAFM (Computer Aided Facility Management) software system and maintain the necessary records, ensuring they are accurate and up to date (full training will be provided) * Comply with Health and Safety, Environmental and Quality legislative requirements * Contribute to and achieve Safety, Quality, Compliance and Continuous Improvement targets * Undertake ad\-hoc projects and repairs on occasion as required and within individuals' competence. * Ensure any training deemed necessary by your line manager is completed in a timely manner and be open to CPD requirements. * Must be prepared to take on and become familiar with all relevant site systems as needed to perform the job in a safe and efficient manner * Be flexible in your approach to allow for and adapt to the evolving needs of the site, client and company. * Act as a brand ambassador and present themselves in a neat and tidy manner. * Wear and keep issued uniforms in an appropriate manner for an office environment. * Maintain Company assets such as tools and plant in safe working order and report defects to supervisor. * Willing to support colleagues toward the end of achieving team goals. * Maintain and use appropriately PPE issued and report defects or additional PPE requirements to supervisor. * Any other duties as deemed necessary for the efficiency of the business **Qualifications, Experience \& Knowledge:** **Required** * Good command of the English Language and ability to write/fill reports and update CAFM system * Manual Handling Certificate or training The following would be an advantage * SOLAS Safe Pass * Health \& Safety qualifications such as IOSH or evidence of H\&S training * Experience as part of a "Moves and Changes" (MAC) team. * ECDL or other Basic IT qualifications such as Microsoft Certs.

title: Power Platform Developer
description: Our Dublin\-based client is looking for a Power Platform Developer to join their team. They are seeking a developer who can enhance and expand their digital solutions landscape through the design, development, and support of Microsoft Power Platform applications, integrating with Microsoft 365 technologies including SharePoint and Dynamics 365\. This position is with a well\-established, market\-leading organisation. For this role, you will be required to work on\-site. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, develop, and maintain solutions using the Microsoft Power Platform, including Power Apps, Power Automate, and Power BI. * Build and customise Canvas Apps and Model\-Driven Apps to support and improve business processes. * Develop automated workflows and integrations using Power Automate. * Collaborate with title: Practice Accountant
description: We are currently hiring for a leading accountancy firm in Kildare. specialising. * Comfortable working in a supporting, cross\-functional role, collaborating with stakeholders across the organisation **Benefits** * Healthcare cover through the VHI * Company pension title: Private Equity Investment Associate
description: **Job Title:** Private Equity Investment Associate **Sector:** Private Equity **Location:** Dublin **Salary:** DOE plus bonus and benefits **Our Client** Our client is a highly regarded Private Equity firm with an exceptional track record of partnering with ambitious businesses and supporting them through periods of growth and transformation. Following continued expansion and a strong pipeline of transactions, they are seeking to appoint a Private Equity Investment Associate to join their high\-calibre investment team. **Why should you apply?** This is a rare opportunity to join one of the most respected investment platforms in the Irish market. Working alongside experienced investors and entrepreneurs, you will gain exposure across the full investment lifecycle including origination, execution, portfolio management and value creation initiatives. The role offers outstanding exposure to senior decision\-makers, management teams and investment committees while providing an exceptional platform for long\-term career development. external advisers. • Portfolio Value Creation – Partner with management teams to monitor performance and support strategic initiatives. • Market Research – Identify industry trends, competitive dynamics and emerging investment opportunities. • Stakeholder Management – Develop relationships with management teams, advisers, lenders and key market participants. • Investment Committee Support – Prepare investment papers, presentations and materials to support internal decision\-making processes. **Interested in this position?** To apply please submit your CV to Lisa Tully who is managing this assignment via the link below or to inquire further please contact us directly on 01 529 4200\.

title: Proprietary Trader
description: Job Title: Proprietary Trader Location: Denver, CO; Los Angeles, CA; Charlotte, NC; Baltimore, MD; Philadelphia, PA; Tampa, FL **Job Description:** **T3 Global Group, LLC** is currently seeking skilled and **experienced traders** to join our team. As a Trader at T3 Global, you will play a vital role. You will have the opportunity to control your own trading strategies and make independent decisions to maximize profitability while being a part of a seasoned trading community. **About:** T3 Global Group, LLC is a comprehensive destination for institutional traders and trading teams aiming to optimize their performance in the current financial markets. Our dedication lies in offering professional traders a top\-tier infrastructure within a professional setting. Designed with a focus on enabling professionals to effectively scale their trading businesses, T3 includes a diverse community of individuals with backgrounds in investment banking, quantitative trading, private equity, hedge funds, and other non\-finance fields. We seek both individuals and trading teams with an entrepreneurial spirit, strong work ethic, exceptional business instincts and a demonstrated ability to make sound decisions under pressure. T3 Global currently trades Futures, Equities and Options. **Benefits:** * Access to T3's Balance Sheet: Leverage our financial strength to bolster your trading strategies. * Professional\-Grade Trading Software: Utilize cutting\-edge technology designed for professional traders. * Customizable Routing: Tailor your trade executions to fit your strategies with our flexible routing options. * Stock Locates and Loans: Find and finance the shares you need with our comprehensive stock locate and loan capabilities. * Elite Trading Community: Engage with a network of professional traders for live trade discussions, market analysis, and real\-time trade ideas. **Responsibilities:** * Execute trades in accordance with established trading strategies and risk management guidelines. * Monitor market conditions, analyze data, and identify trading opportunities. * Conduct thorough research and analysis to develop and refine trading strategies. * Utilize advanced trading tools and technology to execute trades efficiently. * Monitor and manage trade positions, including risk exposure and portfolio performance. * Stay informed about market trends, economic news, and industry developments that may impact trading strategies. * Collaborate with other traders and teams to share insights and optimize trading performance. * Continuously evaluate and improve trading strategies based on market conditions and performance analysis. * Adhere to regulatory and compliance guidelines relevant to trading activities. * Maintain accurate records of trades, positions, and performance metrics. * First loss capital. **Requirements:** * Bachelor's degree in finance, economics, mathematics, or a related field (or equivalent experience). * Proven track record of successful trading in one or more financial markets. * Strong understanding of trading principles, market dynamics, and risk management strategies. * Proficient in utilizing trading platforms and tools to execute trades. * Excellent analytical skills and the ability to interpret complex data and make informed trading decisions. * Demonstrated ability to work effectively under pressure and make sound decisions in fast\-paced trading environments. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate and share insights with other traders. * High level of self\-motivation, discipline, and resilience. * Flexibility to adapt to changing market conditions and trading strategies. Please note, a background check and additional documents are required prior to joining. Compensation is performance based. If you are passionate about trading, possess a strong work ethic, and thrive in a dynamic trading environment, we encourage you to apply for the Trader position at T3 Global.

title: Quality and Patient Safety Coordinator – special projects
description: **Purpose of role** The purpose of this post is to work with the Quality and Patient Safety team, General Manager and Director of Quality and Patient Safety on all relevant activities in the delivery of QPS operations within Children’s Health Ireland. This will include day to day service delivery within the QPS directorate, as well as management of special projects related to internal and external reviews and enquiries, and integration with the HSE. **Essential Criteria** * A third level qualification (minimum Level 8 on QQI framework) in healthcare, quality, patient safety or related field * A minimum of 3\-5 years’ relevant experience in a healthcare setting * Good working knowledge of incident and risk management processes, audit, standards and quality improvement within the Irish healthcare setting * Excellent ICT skills **How To Apply And Informal Enquiries** Applications for this post must be accompanied by a cover letter, setting out relevant experience that illustrates how the essential criteria listed above is met. The criterion for short listing is based on the requirements of the post, as outlined in the eligibility criteria. * Please note that you must submit a cover letter with your CV, this forms part of your application and CV’s will not be accepted without a detailed cover letter. The closing date for submissions of CV’s and cover letter is **3rd June 2026 by5 pm.** Applications must be completed through the advertised post on **CHI.jobs** by clicking **‘Apply for Job’.** **Applications will not be accepted through direct email or any other method.** For informal enquiries for this specialty/department, please contact Claire Collins, QPS Programme Manager, Claire.Collins@childrenshealthireland.ie or (087\) 3838552\. For other queries relating to this recruitment process, please contact Talent Acquisition Specialist – Taurai machuwe at taurai.machuwe@childrenshealthireland.ie **PLEASE NOTE:** CHI has transitioned to a process of a one commencement day per month for all new employees, CHI internal transfers and Secondments. This update to our Onboarding process is aligned to changes in our monthly/fortnightly payroll and with the launch of our new corporate induction program. This process enhancement ensures that we can thoroughly prepare for your arrival and facilitate a smooth transition in your onboarding journey. **It is important for you to note that if you do not have your pre\-employments and mandatory training completed in time, your commencement date will be deferred to the next available date.** Below, you’ll find the list of **commencement dates for 2026** for your information. * 7 September 2026 * 5 October 2026 * 9 November 2026

description: Back to search results R\&D Operations Engineer Graduate Location: Gent, BE Poznan, PL Lezo, ES Soliera, IT Krakow, PL Zlotow, PL Prostejov, CZ Rome, IT Froges, FR Logrono, ES Seville, ES Wroclaw, PL Lisbon, PT Naples, IT Lugo, IT Prague, CZ Bologna, IT Arenzano, IT Madrid, ES Gdansk, PL Florence, IT Alzira, ES Venturina, IT Milan, IT Dublin, IE Sligo, IE Piatra Neamt, RO Burgos, ES, CP 09001 Turin, IT Barcelona, ES Lodz, PL Porto, PT Novy Bydzov, CZ Braga, PT Department: R\&D/Innovation Management Full\-time or Part\-time: Full\-Time Job Type: Employee Shift: On site **Accelerate the possible by joining a winning Amcor team that’s transforming the packaging industry and improving lives around the world.** At Amcor, we unpack possibility through our innovative and responsible packaging to provide solutions that benefit our customers, our people and our planet. More than 10,000 consumers worldwide encounter our products every second and rely on us for safe access to food, medicine and other goods. We value their trust by making safety our guiding principle. It’s our core value and integral to how we do business. Beyond this core principle, our shared values and behaviors unite us as we work together to elevate customers, shape lives and protect the future. We champion our customers and help them succeed. We play to win – adapting quickly in an everchanging world – and make smart choices to safeguard our business, our communities and the people we serve for generations to come. And we invest in our world\-class team, empowering our colleagues to unpack their potential, because we believe when our people grow, so does our business. To learn more about playing for Team Amcor, visit www.amcor.com I LinkedIn I Glassdoor I Facebook I YouTube Think BIG. Start small. Move fast. Go Far. Amcor Europe **R\&D Operations Engineer Graduate Program** Each year we are looking for a group of Graduates interested joining our Graduate Program in various functions: R\&D, Operations, Supply Chain, Sales \& Marketing, HR, Finance, PMO and more. **The next program will start in October 2026 and will contain 3 international rotations across Amcor plants \& offices in Europe. The contract location will depend on the successful candidate.** **Take on a Challenge!** As an AFEMEA Graduate you will gain insight into the assigned function by being involved in various central European projects, together with your Manager and Supervisors. You will have a chance to prove your skills on different levels, getting involved with various projects and collaborating with numerous stakeholders with different background, experience, and seniority. Our Graduate Program is designed for creative and motivated thinkers who love learning and taking on new challenges and responsibilities, not afraid of dealing with ambiguity and change. In your role as **R\&D Operations Engineer Graduate** , you will be : * Supporting the development, deployment, and continuous improvement of R\&D systems, tools, and operational processes across Europe * Contributing to cross\-functional European projects with other functions (Supply Chain, Procurement, Sales, IT, Sustainability, Operations, Product Management) * Interfacing between business stakeholders and system/process owners to translate operational needs into structured, practical solutions * Aligning data and analytics initiatives with business priorities to enable fact\-based decision\-making * Supporting change management activities by preparing \& delivering documentation, training materials, and communication for new or improved systems and processes * Rotating across projects and focus areas within R\&D Operations to explore interests, develop strengths, and shape future career direction **What do you need** **?** **Education \& Experience** * Master’s/Honours degree in Industrial Engineering, General Engineering, Data Engineering or Chemical Engineering * Evidence of academic achievements and/or extracurricular activities * Up to 2 years of relevant internship or work experience in an industrial, engineering, R\&D, or operations environment * First international study or work experience as an asset * Exposure to cross‑functional projects, data analysis, or process improvement activities is an advantage **Technical \& Analytical Skills** * Strong analytical and structured problem‑solving mindset, with the ability to understand end‑to‑end processes * Interest in IT systems and data, with basic knowledge of SQL, programming concepts, databases, and/or data modelling * Experience with ERP systems (e.g. SAP) and/or CRM tools (e.g. Salesforce) is a plus * Proficiency in MS Excel and PowerPoint; Power BI is nice to have **Personal \& Professional Skills** * Ability to see the big picture and understand connections between processes, systems, and functions * Curious, proactive, and motivated to drive continuous improvement * Strong communication and collaboration skills in international, multidisciplinary teams * Detail‑oriented, well‑organized, and result‑focused * Flexible, adaptable and ready to embrace a change **Language \& Mobility** * Fluency in English. Any other European language as an advantage * Willingness to travel and work abroad across Europe * Permanent work permit in the EU **Why choose the Amcor Experience?** The Amcor Graduate Program is an exciting **18 months opportunity** to further develop your skills and unwrap your expertise with an international packaging leader offering you a unique working experience in an innovative and diverse environment. **We will accept only applications in English.** **About Amcor** Amcor is the global leader in developing and producing responsible consumer packaging and dispensing solutions across a variety of materials for nutrition, health, beauty and wellness categories. Our global product innovation and sustainability expertise enables us to solve packaging challenges around the world every day, producing a range of flexible packaging, rigid packaging, cartons and closures that are more sustainable, functional and appealing for our customers and their consumers. We are guided by our purpose of elevating customers, shaping lives and protecting the future. Supported by a commitment to safety, over 75,000 people generate $23 billion in annualized sales from operations that span over 400 locations in more than 40 countries. title: Rack Decomissioning Technician , DCC Communities, DECOM
description: **Description** The Decommissioning Technical team is responsible for processing and facilitating large scale legacy Data Center equipment through a liquidation process to ensure that targeted equipment is ready to be removed off premises. Team members will also process large volumes of customer data to ensure that all customer data is sanitized and properly managed. This position requires candidates to be a self\-starter, highly motivated individual who can adhere to strict processes and be able to perform repetitive tasks with no deviation and demonstrate continuously consistent results. Team Members will be working in a team environment not only with other members of the team but also from other teams and must be able to communicate and promote cross\-team functionality. This is an entry level position and qualified candidates do not require intimate details of software operations or networking protocols. Amazon Data Centers takes customer data with the utmost importance, and selected candidates must be able to perform repetitive tasks without variance and adhere to strict Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) that ensures customer data is protected and properly handled. Key job responsibilities **Data Center Technicians (Decommission Technician) Will Be Responsible For** Decommissioning and processing of legacy hardware out of the data center facilities Moving/lifting packages usually no greater than 75 lbs. Unpacking/repacking fragile electronics Relocating and moving of large enterprise equipment Creating/attaching labels and barcodes Hard drive installation and removal, and destruction Basic Networking removal/installation and clearing of configurations. Physical layer and Application layer Updating progress through a web ticketing system Reporting of processes and regular status updates Documenting procedures Escalating to senior technicians and management as needed/required **Familiarity With Basic PC Components/hardware** * HDD/SSD/Flash/Removable storage media * USB/FireWire/Esata * Ram/CPU/Motherboard/Input\-Output devices There may be an element of shift based work required with this role. A day in the life AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. **About The Team** About AWS Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve. **Basic Qualifications** * High school or equivalent diploma * Experience with computer hardware troubleshooting and repair * Experience in networking **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience dealing effectively with customers during problem resolution and operating efficiently under pressure Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. title: Recreational Coordinator
description: Cheeverstown is seeking an enthusiastic, creative and motivated Recreational Co\-Ordinator to join our team. This exciting role will support adults with intellectual disabilities to access meaningful opportunities, build relationships, participate in their communities, and enjoy fulfilling social, recreational and leisure experiences. Working across Respite and New Directions Services, you will play a key role in developing person\-centred programmes that promote inclusion, independence, active citizenship, human rights, and community participation. **About The Role** As Recreational Co\-Ordinator, you will lead and coordinate engaging activities and opportunities that reflect the interests, goals and aspirations of the people we support. You will work closely with individuals, families, multidisciplinary teams and community organisations to ensure meaningful participation and positive outcomes. This role is ideal for someone who is passionate about social inclusion, advocacy, community development, sport, recreation and person\-centred practice. Key Responsibilities Person\-Centred Support * Develop and coordinate meaningful recreational, social and community\-based activities. * Promote choice, independence and active participation. * Support individuals to achieve personal goals and develop new skills. * Actively advocate for the rights and inclusion of people supported by Cheeverstown. * Build strong relationships with families, community groups and local organisations. Community Engagement * Facilitate access to local community activities and events. * Coordinate social groups, friendship networks and community\-based opportunities. * Support participation in Special Olympics training and competitions. * Develop partnerships within local communities through networking and collaboration. * Identify opportunities to enhance social inclusion and community participation. Programme Development * Assist with planning and developing innovative recreational programmes. * Complete assessments and gather information to ensure supports are tailored to individual needs. * Work collaboratively with Respite and New Directions teams. * Support service development initiatives and partnership opportunities. **Essential Requirements** * Relevant qualification in Social Care, Health Care, Community Development, Recreation, Disability Studies or a related discipline. * Minimum 2 years' experience supporting people with intellectual disabilities. * Experience working collaboratively with individuals, families and multidisciplinary teams. * Strong organisational, planning and coordination skills. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to work independently and use initiative. * Strong IT skills, including Microsoft Office. * Full clean driving licence. * Commitment to person\-centred practice, human rights and community inclusion. Desirable * Experience coordinating community programmes, sports, social activities or events. * Knowledge of New Directions and person\-centred planning. * Experience supporting Special Olympics or similar recreational initiatives. * Experience in advocacy and community networking.

title: Repair Technician
description: Overview: Repair Technician I Location: Dundalk, Ireland Salary: €30160 to 45,240 WHO ARE WE: Ever wonder who brings the entertainment to your flights? Panasonic Avionics Corporation is \#1 in the industry for delivering inflight products such as movies, games, WiFi, and now Bluetooth headphone connectivity! As a Maintenance Services Representative for PAC, you will service our electronic aviation devices onboard planes! How exciting would it be to be a part of the technology that delights millions of people in an industry that’s here to stay! If you want to learn more about us visit us at www.panasonic.aero. And for a full listing of open job opportunities go to www.panasonic.aero/join\-us/. Responsibilities: JOB SUMMARY We are seeking an entry\-level Technician to support the testing, troubleshooting, repair, and calibration of electronic units (LRUs) using approved procedures, tools, and test equipment. The successful candidate will accurately document all work within the company control system while maintaining the highest standards of safety, quality, and compliance. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with a keen interest in electronics, engineering, or mechanical assembly who is looking to build a long\-term technical career. Full training will be provided, enabling you to develop hands\-on skills in testing, fault diagnosis, calibration, and repair within a supportive and professional environment. MAJOR RESPONSIBILITIES. * Perform basic repairs on electronic units, including component replacement and restoration of functionality, under the guidance of experienced technicians. * Assist with the setup, testing, and calibration of electronic units in accordance with approved procedures. * Conduct basic testing and fault\-finding activities to identify straightforward issues, escalating more complex faults as required. * Accurately record repair activities, parts usage, test results, and discrepancies within the company management system. * Assemble and install electronic and mechanical components using techniques such as soldering, crimping, fastening, and bonding (full training provided). * Install repaired assemblies into housings, cabinets, or equipment in line with work instructions and technical documentation. * Prepare repair cost quotation worksheets using established processes and guidelines. * Follow maintenance manuals, technical drawings, and repair documentation to support repair and testing activities. * Maintain a clean, organised, and safe working environment while adhering to all quality, safety, and compliance requirements. * Continuously develop technical knowledge and practical skills through on\-the\-job training and mentoring. Qualifications: EDUCATION/EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS * High School Diploma or equivalent qualification * Recognized technical qualification or relevant experience. * Demonstrable experience in an electronic systems repair environment. KNOWLEDGE/SKILL REQUIREMENTS * Ability to learn quickly and adapt to new processes, tools, and technologies within a highly regulated aerospace environment. * Demonstrated commitment to quality, safety, compliance, and continuous professional development. * Effective communication skills in English, both written and verbal, with the ability to work collaboratively in a multicultural team environment. * Basic understanding of electronic circuits and troubleshooting principles. * Ability to interpret and follow engineering documentation, maintenance manuals, work instructions, and technical procedures. * Familiarity with common electronic test equipment, including multimeters, power supplies, and oscilloscopes, gained through education, training, or practical experience. * Ability to work with small electromechanical components and use hand and power tools safely and effectively. * Willingness to develop skills in testing, calibration, fault diagnosis, and repair through structured training. Effectively communicate with shop supervision, Engineering and Quality departments to solve technical repair problems. OTHER REQUIREMENTS * Ability to travel up to 10\-20% of the time to domestic and/or international locations. * Must have a working knowledge of Microsoft Office applications. * Acquires job skills and learns company policies and procedures to complete routine tasks. * Works on assignments that are routine in nature, requiring limited judgment. * May receive detailed instructions on work. Candidates must have right to work. Annual Salary plus benefits on offer for this position. Panasonic is committed to adhering to EU employment laws and regulations. We are an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender identity, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, and any other characteristic protected by EU law or company policy. Individuals who meet the qualifications are expected to perform essential job functions with or without reasonable accommodation. Pre\-employment drug testing is required for safety\-sensitive positions or as may be otherwise mandated by contract or EU law. Due to the substantial volume of responses, we will only be able to respond to candidates of interest. All candidates must possess valid authorization to work in the applicable EU countries. We appreciate your interest in Panasonic Corporation of North America.

title: Research Intern Vacancy
company: Institute of International and European Affairs
description: The Institute of International and European Affairs (IIEA), a leading independent think\-tank based in Dublin, Ireland, is opening a competition for the position of Research Intern to support the IIEA’s research and events during the Irish Presidency of the Council of the EU. This internship would be for a duration of up to 6 months, depending on candidate availability and start date, and would conclude on 31 December 2026\. This internship would be ideal for recent graduates seeking experience in international affairs. Research Interns at the IIEA work as part of the IIEA research team, reporting directly to the Director of Research, and may be asked to assist on any or all of the following tasks: providing research support for briefings, blogs and other written materials; assisting with event planning and organisation; helping to coordinate a working group comprising leading experts from across the IIEA’s range of research areas; and facilitating the IIEA’s relations with key Irish and EU stakeholders, including government departments, EU institutions, NGOs, and universities. The successful candidate(s) will have a unique opportunity to immerse themselves in EU and international policy research; engage with leaders in policy, politics and business; assist in the creation and execution of programmes of research; assist with events featuring some of the highest profile speakers in Ireland and internationally; and build a network of contacts in Irish and European policy circles. The deadline for applications is Friday 26 June 2026\. **Responsibilities** The main responsibilities of this position include: * Tracking and analysing significant developments in specific EU policy areas, EU institutions, and EU politics * Providing research assistance for analytical papers, blogs, and explainers on themes related to EU and international affairs * Liaising, when required, with IIEA members, speakers, and guests * Providing assistance in the organising of programmes of keynote addresses, seminars, roundtable discussions, and workshops with high\-level policymakers and thought leaders as part of the IIEA's events programme **Requirements:** The ideal candidate would have * First\-class or high second\-class honours in a field such as International Relations, Public Policy, European Studies, Economics, Law, Political Economy, Political Science, etc. (Candidates who have not yet received their final results may be asked to provide academic references.) * Good knowledge of Irish politics and EU institutions * Excellent research, writing, editing, and proofing skills * Ability to meet deadlines * Attention to detail, accuracy, and efficiency * Ability to multitask and strong organisational skills * Excellent interpersonal and oral and written communication skills **Application details:** * To apply please send a CV and cover letter with the subject line “Research Intern – Application” to jobs@iiea.com * LinkedIn applications will not be considered * Due to the expected volume of applications, we will only be able to respond to applicants who are selected for interview * Canvassing will result in disqualification * Please note that this role is Dublin\-based and it is expected that the successful candidate would primarily work from the IIEA’s offices at 8 North Great George’s Street, Dublin 1\. **The salary for this internship is €32,000 per annum *pro rata* .**

title: Residential Rehabilitation Assistant
description: Are you passionate about making a real difference in people's lives? We're looking for a compassionate and dedicated **Residential Rehabilitation Assistants** to join our dynamic team in our **Eaglewood, Rochestown Avenue Service** , where person\-centred care is at the heart of everything we do. **About The Role:** As a Residential Rehabilitation Assistant, you will play a key part in supporting adults with an acquired brain injury to live with greater independence, confidence, and autonomy. Working as part of a dedicated interdisciplinary team, you will help persons served build daily living skills, engage in meaningful activities, and work toward their personal rehabilitation goals. This role is a hands\-on, varied, and deeply rewarding – every day you will contribute to creating a safe, supportive, person\-centred environment where individuals are empowered to make choices and participate fully in their community. **The contract available is:** * 3x Specified Purpose, Full\-Time, 39 hours contracts The salary range will be 31,550 \- 45,381 depending on experience. This will be pro\-rated based on working 39 hours per week. **What You'll Be Doing:** * Build respectful, trusting relationships and offer consistent support * Help create and guide personalised rehabilitation plans * Encourage independence through daily living skills, personal care, and goal\-focused activities * Work closely with the team, attend meetings, and keep progress updated on IPlanit * Adhere to professional standards, policies, and procedures * Assist persons served with budgeting, money management and disability benefits where applicable in line with Organisational policy * Maintain safe, secure, and healthy work environment * For a more detailed list of role duties, please review the attached job specification **What We're Looking For:** We are seeing a compassionate support professional who believes in the rights, dignity, and potential of every person. You will bring empathy, patience, and a genuine commitment to person\-centred practice. The ideal candidate is proactive, reliable, and comfortable working broth independently and as part of a collaborative team. If you value meaningful connections, empower others to lead fulfilling lives, and want to be part of a supportive team \- this could be the perfect role for you. **Must Haves:** * Relevant Health or Social Care qualification to a QQI Level 5 or higher * Full driving licence * Person\-centred values * Strong communication skills \- be able to engage compassionately, clearly and confidently in spoken and written English * Confident using basic IT systems to stay connected and organised **Great To Have:** * Experience in Residential or Community settings * Background in Disability or Illness support **Why Join Us?** * Making a Real Impact \- Be part of a mission\-driven team passionately advocating for supporting people impacted by brain injury to rebuild their lives with dignity * Grow with Us \- Access tailored induction and specialist training, plus meaningful career development opportunities to help you reach your potential * Feel Valued \- Enjoy long service awards, premium pay (where applicable), paid maternity leave, and a supportive culture that celebrates your contribution * Work\-Life Balance Matters \- Flexible working options and wellbeing initiatives empower you to live fully\- at work and beyond * Financial Security \& Perks \- Competitive salary, defined contribution pension scheme (for eligible employees), free life cover, and access to the Health Care Staff Credit Union * Support When You Need It \- Benefit from the Employee Assistance Programme, revenue\-approved mileage rates, and the Bike to Work Scheme If you’re ready to step into a role that balances professional impact with personal purpose, we’d love to hear from you. You're welcome to get in touch with informal queries by emailing the Hiring Manager. Fiona Doyle \- Local Service Manager – 086 8515275 \- fdoyle@abiireland.ie Please apply by 2nd July 2026 at 5pm!

title: Respiratory Physiologist
description: **Advertisement** Applications are invited for the position of: **Respiratory Physiologist** **Position** **Permanent** **Essential Criteria:** Possess the BSc in Clinical Measurement from Dublin Institute of Technology, BSc Hons Health Physiology from Ulster University or equivalent as confirmed by the Irish Institute of Clinical Measurement Science (IICMS). **Or** Possess the Certificate in Medical Physics and Psychological Measurement (MPPM) from Dublin Institute of Technology or equivalent as confirmed by the Irish Institute of Clinical Measurement Science (IICMS). **Or** Possess an equivalent relevant scientific qualification in Science from (Level 8\) as confirmed by the Irish Institute of Clinical Measurements Science (IICMS). **And** Candidates must have the clinical, managerial, and administrative capacity to properly discharge the functions of the role. **Desirable Criteria:** Have at least 1 year post graduate. \-\- For Informal enquiries please contact Anita Doggett\- Judge, Head of Service, Respiratory SVPH \- **anitadoggettjudge@svhg.ie** Interested candidates should apply by uploading an up\-to\-date curriculum vitae. Closing date for applications: **5pm 3rd July, 2026\.**
